steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two parts joined by 'however.' First: some researchers posit that species on Grande Terre belong to clades predating Gondwana's split ~80 million years ago. Second: Sundue et al.'s study found crown ages of only 8.5–10 million years. The structure is an idea followed by contradicting evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It presents a possibility that some researchers have raised, then describes a study that clarifies their reasons for doing so.: the study does not clarify the researchers' reasons — it contradicts their conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — It explains a view some researchers have advanced, then discusses study results that led them to reconsider that view.: the text never says the researchers reconsidered their view; it only presents the incompatible findings. ✗
  • Choice C — It identifies a discrepancy between a hypothesis some researchers have proposed and related research findings, then explains that discrepancy.: the text identifies the discrepancy but never explains why it exists. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes an idea that some researchers have put forward, then presents study results that are incompatible with that idea.: the first part describes the researchers' posit (clades predate Gondwana split, ~80 mya); the second presents Sundue et al.'s crown-age findings (8.5–10 mya) which are incompatible. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
